description: Generate a deterministic synthetic 3-store weekly retail dataset (24-week context + 12-week horizon) with price/promotion/holiday/day-of-week covariates, decompose each covariate's effect on sales, and export a 108-row CSV plus a metadata JSON describing the XReg (forecast_with_covariates) workflow.
rules: takes no inputs — data is generated with a fixed RNG seed (42), so output is reproducible
pure numpy/pandas/matplotlib; the TimesFM XReg API is only printed, never invoked
covariate effects are fixed: holiday +200, promotion +150, price -20/$ above base
writes sales_with_covariates.csv (108 rows) and covariates_metadata.json to record_store
usage: No vars required. Output: sales_with_covariates.csv (3 stores x 36 weeks = 108 rows) and covariates_metadata.json (dimensions, covariate roles, effect magnitudes).
limitation: Demonstration data only — does not run real TimesFM inference. Use forecast-csv for actual forecasts. Store price ordering is store_A > store_B > store_C by construction.
